title: Entropy-Regularized Differentiable Top-k Attention: Ablation and Benchmarking for Sink Mitigation and Adaptive Sparsity

datasets.name: LRA (Long Range Arena)
datasets.description: Standard benchmark suite for long-context sequence modeling, including ListOps, Text, Retrieval, Image, and Pathfinder tasks.
datasets.size_info: 5 tasks; max seq len = 4K–16K tokens; total train examples ≈ 1.2M
datasets.preprocessing_notes: Use official tokenization and preprocessing scripts; pad/truncate to task-specific max lengths; apply no data augmentation.

datasets.name: LongBench
datasets.description: Comprehensive long-context evaluation benchmark with 6 NLP tasks (e.g., multi-hop QA, summarization, code generation) and 3 domain-specific variants (legal, scientific, conversational).
datasets.size_info: 25k+ test instances across 9 tasks; context lengths 4K–32K; human-annotated ground truth.
datasets.preprocessing_notes: Use official v1.0 splits; retain full context without truncation; apply task-specific prompt templates; evaluate with exact match & ROUGE-L where applicable.

datasets.name: PG-19
datasets.description: Large-scale book-length language modeling dataset with documents averaging ~100K tokens; stresses memory and attention distribution over extreme contexts.
datasets.size_info: 28.5K books; avg. length ≈ 102K tokens; train/test split provided.
datasets.preprocessing_notes: Chunk into non-overlapping 32K-token sequences; use byte-level BPE tokenizer from GPT-2; discard sequences < 8K tokens.

baselines.name: Dense Transformer (Vanilla)
baselines.description: Standard full self-attention with softmax, no sparsity.

baselines.name: Top-k Sparse Attention
baselines.description: Hard top-k selection on attention logits followed by softmax; k=64 or adaptive per-head.

baselines.name: Gated Sparse Attention (GSA)
baselines.description: Post-attention sigmoid gating applied to dense attention output before value projection; sparsity induced *after* softmax.

baselines.name: DynamicViT
baselines.description: Token-level dynamic sparsification via confidence-based pruning of redundant tokens before attention.

proposed_method.name: Entropy-Regularized Differentiable Top-k (ER-DTopk)
proposed_method.description: A fully differentiable sparse attention mechanism that integrates gating-aware logits, trainable temperature τ, and entropy regularization into the top-k selection loop — enabling end-to-end optimization of sparsity-accuracy trade-offs while eliminating attention sinks.
key_components: Logit-level gating: element-wise multiplication of attention logits with sigmoid-gated mask derived from token-wise features (e.g., norm, positional encoding, or lightweight MLP)
Differentiable top-k: soft top-k operator using torchsort or custom CUDA kernel with temperature τ (trainable scalar per layer)
Entropy regularization: KL divergence penalty between attention distribution and uniform prior, scaled by λ (trainable per-layer scalar)
Sink mitigation objective: explicit gradient signal against low-entropy attention maps via entropy loss term
proposed_method.architecture: Drop-in replacement for standard MultiHeadAttention. For each head: (1) compute QK^T → logits; (2) apply logit-level gating mask; (3) scale by trainable τ; (4) apply differentiable top-k to produce sparse attention weights; (5) compute entropy loss; (6) apply softmax over selected indices only; (7) attend over values. All components are differentiable and jointly trained.

metrics.name: Accuracy
metrics.description: Task-specific accuracy (e.g., classification accuracy for ListOps, EM for QA in LongBench, PPL for PG-19).

metrics.name: FLOPs
metrics.description: Total floating-point operations per forward pass (normalized per token), measured via fvcore or PyTorch profiler.

metrics.name: Memory_GB
metrics.description: Peak GPU memory consumption during training (batch size = 8, seq len = 8K).

metrics.name: Latency_ms
metrics.description: End-to-end inference latency (ms) per sequence on A100 (batch=1, seq=8K).

metrics.name: Sink_Entropy_Bits
metrics.description: Mean Shannon entropy (bits) of attention distributions across all heads and layers; higher = less sink formation.

metrics.name: Sparsity_Ratio
metrics.description: Fraction of attention weights pruned (i.e., zeroed) per head, averaged across layers and batches.

ablation_groups.description: Tests whether gating *before* top-k (logit-level) is necessary vs. post-softmax gating.

variants.name: ER-DTopk (logit-gating)
variants.description: Full proposed method: gating applied to logits before differentiable top-k.

variants.name: ER-DTopk (post-softmax gating)
variants.description: Gating applied after softmax, as in [25]; same entropy regularization and differentiable top-k.

variants.name: ER-DTopk (no gating)
variants.description: Baseline differentiable top-k + entropy regularization, but no gating mask.

ablation_groups.description: Evaluates necessity and sensitivity of entropy regularization for sink mitigation.

variants.name: ER-DTopk (λ=0.0)
variants.description: No entropy regularization; only differentiable top-k + logit gating.

variants.name: ER-DTopk (λ=trainable)
variants.description: Per-layer trainable λ optimized jointly with model.

ablation_groups.description: Compares differentiable vs. hard top-k under identical gating and entropy settings.

variants.name: ER-DTopk (soft-topk)
variants.description: Full differentiable implementation using softmax relaxation or sorting gradients.

variants.name: Hard-Topk + gating + entropy
variants.description: Non-differentiable argtopk with straight-through estimator; otherwise identical architecture.

evidence_summary: No quantitative baseline performance numbers were available in the published quantitative evidence section. All 'expected_performance' values are set to null per instruction, as no numeric results from source papers were provided.
data_provenance_note: All baseline metric values are marked 'N/A' or null because no published quantitative evidence was supplied. Projected improvements for ER-DTopk (e.g., '5–10% higher Sink_Entropy_Bits than GSA', '2–3× lower FLOPs than Dense Transformer at matched accuracy') are *not* included in this JSON — per instructions, only verifiable published numbers may appear in 'expected_performance', and none were provided. Any performance claims for the proposed method will be reported *only* in experimental results post-training, not projected here.
